violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Beverage relocated Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (mandoline) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. Equipment was on clean knife rack. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed it in dishwasher. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in plastic containers greater than four inches deep. PIC relocated to sheet pans as food was cooked less than 2 hours ago. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

ROP seafood was observed thawing with its package in tact. ROP seafood was still partially frozen. Walk-In ambient was observed at 40 degrees. Food was removed from package. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in the walk-in have an accumulation of dust and debris. Shelves to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in shelving have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Shelving to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

Various foods in the prep unit were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food held in unit for greater than 4 hours was disposed of. Food recently placed in unit was moved to other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen was observed propped open. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Door was shut. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024
The prep unit was not in good repair. Ambient temperature was observed @ 50 degrees. Unit was taken out of service. Unit to not be used until serviced/ repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

The non food contact surfaces of the following was observed with various debris: The shelf above the prep unit had an accumulation of dust and debris. The shelf inside of the prep unit had an accumulation of food debris. The shelving next to the dish machine had an accumulation of food debris. The ice machine was observed with an accumulation of bio-film. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024

An employee was observed washing their hands at the food prep sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Crab Rangoon and shrimp was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC moved items to a shallow pan to allow proper air circulation/cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Beef was observed thawing in the food prep sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2023
The following were observed improperly stored: 1) Knife stored inbetween unclean surfaces of prep units 2) Bowls used as scoops in dry ingredients New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in the walk-in coolers, inside the refrigeration prep units, and hood vent system have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2023
The floors in the dry storage area has an accumulation of food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2023
